alt: Photo by Ali Abdaal in Kew Gardens with @izzysealey.

caption:
How do you expand your personal brand into new topics? 🤔

Short answer: you just start talking about new stuff.

When I transitioned from medicine to productivity and tech, some people unsubscribed or unfollowed. Some left comments like “bro, stick to medical school.” That was 8 years ago.

Here’s what I’ve learned since then 👇

1️⃣ You can always find a storyline that makes it make sense, but you don’t have to. Most people aren’t thinking about your personal brand nearly as much as you are.

2️⃣ Platforms are more algorithmic now. Your videos from 5 years ago aren’t getting the same views as your stuff today. So it almost doesn’t matter if the topic has changed.

3️⃣ Audience churn is real. A lot of people following me today weren’t following me 7 years ago. You’re always speaking to a moving crowd.

The pressure you feel to stay “consistent” with your brand is probably, mostly in your head.

You can kind of do what you want 😅

What topic have you been wanting to talk about but felt weird about? 👇

latestComments.text: Because Everybody Doesn't Want to Work they just want success and @aliabdaal you're really a great mentor May God Give you a Long Life.

Like Hormozi would say, no one needs to hit their target every time. You just need one win (few in this context) to be successful. The majority of your repetitions will serve as trial runs where you build muscle memory, resilience and confidence. 💁🏻‍♂️

The thing that @aliabdaal is in the educational space, not the entertainment space. These are two very different games on YouTube. His niche is also very specific, so not everyone will be interested in his content. But more than that, and most importantly, views alone are vanity metrics. You have to look at click through rates,  sales conversions, view quality etc. People often look at their finances the same way. Revenue does matter, but it is vanity. What many businesses fail to look at are: profit margins, ROI over the long term, strong customer retention and positive cashflow. We have to seperate optics from real value.
I’ve counted all your wealth and saw that you only hit $10M in profits this year after almost 10 years of your entrepreneurial journey. Why did it take you so long and why are you broke? Answer me.

caption:
I've been using @claudeai for 2 years, but Claude Code and Claude Co-work are on another level.

Claude Code: I was intimidated at first (terminal = scary), but it's not just for coding. I've connected it to all my files. It knows my projects, goals, and ideas. It can generate content from transcripts, help with my courses, research stuff overnight while I sleep.

Claude Co-work: Same power, better UI. I use it on my Mac to build presentations, do deep research, and search the web.

If you still think AI is just a chatbot on a website … things have changed. It can access your files, run tasks autonomously, and actually do work for you.

Comment CLAUDE and I’ll send you the link to try it out ⬇️

caption:
And then there were three 🐣

Life update: our baby girl arrived to the world after 43 hours labour ❤️ and we’ve been settling into this new season of being a new little family of three 🌟

Motherhood has been the biggest challenge and expansion I’ve experienced in my life so far… both the highest highs and lowest lows, incredible joy and pain and grace and grief and just so much overwhelming love. 

If you’re curious to hear some of my reflections on the experience, I’m documenting navigating the journey of integrating personal growth and purpose with motherhood on both my YouTube (@izzysealey) and my newsletter Letters To My Younger Self (links in bio) 💌✨

Feel free to check out my pregnancy reflections video on YT (filmed when I was 9 months huge haha) - and a post-birth debrief video is coming very soon to a YouTube channel near you… 👀

caption:
3 mistakes that you might be making in your first business 👇

Mistake 1: Not taking action. You're stuck researching, strategising or finding the "perfect" idea. But nothing works unless you put stuff out there. It's easier to steer a car when it's moving.

Mistake 2: Building what YOU want instead of what solves OTHER people's problems. A business solves problems in exchange for money. Have you validated anyone else actually wants your product or service? 🤔

Mistake 3: Not getting mentorship. This doesn’t have to be a person, it can mean books, podcasts, YouTube videos - there's so much free stuff out there. Don't skip the basics and waste years making avoidable mistakes.

Your first idea probably won't make you rich. It's usually the third or fourth pivot that does, so just get started 💪

Comment FREEDOM for a free 1-hour workshop on the foundational principles on how to become financially free ⬇️

caption:
Hot take: outsource the domestic labour you don't enjoy.

Cleaning. Laundry. Cooking. You're probably spending 5-10 hours a week on chores you don't like  and you can probably hire someone to do them for less than your hourly rate.

You can then use those hours for things you actually value like family time, going on a walk, or starting a business on the side. 

If you enjoy the chores, keep doing them. But if you don't? That's the easiest time to buy back.

What would you outsource first? 👇

P.S. I'm hosting a free Spring Reset workshop on April 4th to help you reflect on Q1 and set goals for the next 90 days. Comment SPRING to join 🌸

If you're using an AI every day, stop typing. Start speaking.

Most of us speak way faster than we type. (Even my typing speed is 154 wpm, and I still speak faster.)

The web app I use: Wispr Flow.

Hold the function key → speak → it transcribes instantly wherever your cursor is.

It runs silently in the background. You don't notice it until you need it. And it makes talking to AI feel like actual conversation instead of slow typing.

If you’d like to give Wispr Flow a go, visit wisprflow.ai/ali and use code ALI at checkout, for one month free 🎁

caption:
If you want to actually make money online in 2026, here's the framework 👇

1️⃣ List out your craft skills = things you already know how to do based on your personal, professional, or life experience.

2️⃣ Find a niche. A niche = a person + a problem. Make a list of at least 20-30 combinations of people you could help and problems you could solve.

3️⃣ Go through your list and ask yourself 3 questions for every skill -

🟢 Do I like helping these people?

🟢 Can I actually help them?

🟢 Would they pay at least $2,000 to solve this problem?

If you get green lights on all three questions, you've got a strong business idea that you can start with.

Comment CRAFT and I'll send you a free custom GPT that helps you figure out your craft skills and turn them into a money-making niche 🔥

caption:
It’s official! My first book Feel-Good Productivity is now available to buy around the world 🤯

Humbled and awed by all the support for the book so far. Reminder to please submit your receipt on the form on the website (link in bio) to get your free ticket for my Jan 6th 2024 Annual Planning Workshop. It’s a 3-hour live online workshop where I’ll take you through some great exercises to reflect on 2023, and create goals, plans and system to help make 2024 the best year of your life 😛 you’ve got until Dec 30th to grab a copy if you’d like to attend the Annual Planning Workshop.

Much love ❤️

latestComments.text: The idea that productivity should feel good is such a needed shift. Most people burn out chasing output when the real unlock is designing a year that energizes you. Annual planning workshops like this are proof that the best results come from stepping back and seeing the full picture first.
Proud of you, Ali. Keep up the good work.
Hello Sir, I am Hoorain from Pakistan. I create content about remote jobs for students. I would be honored to interview you for 20 minutes on Zoom. Thank you for inspiring young people.
